compiled_state.title: As a practitioner, consideration interactions become structured acceptance criteria on my epic
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Consideration-level interaction signals from the classifier become structured ACs injected into the epic spec with three required fields, deduplication, and a new provenance category.
**Why**: Makes cross-epic interaction signals actionable by embedding them as verifiable constraints that propagate through the entire planning pipeline.
**Scope**:
- IN: AC structure with 3 required fields, deduplication by shared resource name, "injected" provenance category, approval gate rendering, section placement in epic spec
- OUT: Classification logic (S1), halt/resolution behavior (S3), Playwright distinction (S4)

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, each consideration AC contains: (a) shared resource name, (b) overlapping epic ID+title, (c) falsifiable integration constraint
  <- Satisfies: "Each injected consideration-level acceptance criterion references three required fields"
- When this story is complete, duplicate signals for the same shared resource across multiple overlapping epics are deduplicated to a single AC, using the shared resource name as the deduplication key
  <- Satisfies: "duplicate signals are deduplicated to a single AC"
- When this story is complete, injected ACs carry "injected" provenance and render as bold at the approval gate. The "injected" provenance is applied BEFORE the Phase 3 clean-text strip pass — clean-text strips provenance annotations for the ticket description but the approval gate (which runs before Phase 3) renders them
  <- Satisfies: "injected provenance category extending the existing set"
- When this story is complete, injected ACs appear under a new "## Cross-Epic Interactions" section in the epic spec (separate from Success Criteria) so the SC Gap Check and completion verifier operate on user-authored SCs, while injected ACs are tracked independently
  <- Satisfies: "structured ACs propagate through preplanning to implementation-plan to task execution"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Maintainability] The "injected" provenance extends a 4-category enum (explicit, confirmed-via-gap-question, inferred, researched) — ensure all provenance consumers handle the 5th category, including the approval gate summary line formula
- [Maintainability] The provenance summary line at the approval gate ("N of M criteria confirmed; K inferred requiring review") needs updating to account for the "injected" category count

## Escalation Policy
Escalation policy: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue that could send implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. All assumptions made without escalating are documented.
